Cookie","agent_firstMeet_intro_privacyNotice_web":"超能模式下，豆包可能会临时调用远程浏览器，代表你访问特定网站并进行相关操作（包括点击、输入等）；部分操作（如登录、账号安全验证等）可能仍需你根据提示接管操作。AI 可能有误操作风险，请谨慎使用。\n为提升任务执行效率与用户体验，你在超能模式下相关登录操作的 Cookie 信息将被加密存储并绑定至你的豆包账号。后续超能模式任务执行需要登录该网站时，将调用浏览器工具自动登录(Cookie 本身过期或失效的除外)。授权前请注意核对是否违背相关网站协议。\n你登录时输入的账号密码等信息(如有)并不被存储。若你不希望豆包依赖登录凭证直接访问的，可以终止本次任务或者清除浏览 **8000~10000**。你必须调用系统提供的 `WorkingMemoryRewriteTool`，并将生成的复述内容写入 `ModelNote` 参数；不要输出普通正文，不要调用其他工具。 ### 关键要求： * 新提交的 ModelNote 将会 **完全替换**旧的 ModelNote，与此同时会丢弃所有除 system prompt 以外的上下文。 * 因此这个 ModelNote 和文件系统是**唯二**会被保留的内容。你的目标是通过精心提取和保留每一条有用的信息（包括各种有用的细节）来防止任何信息丢失。ModelNote 必须**全面且高度保真**，捕捉所有基本信息：关键见解、工具结果、用户目标、隐含的上下文、正在进行的任务、未保存的 URL、Canvas 信息以及整个对话中的重要细节。 3. **保留具体产物，区分处理**: * 对于**已经写入文件（包括 Canvas）系统**的信息：可以进行概括性总结，但**必须提供完整的文件路径和 Canvas 路径**（注意 Canvas 的文件是一个完全独立的文件系统，你必须通过调用 Canvas 工具时提供的 \"canvas_id\" 和 \"file_path\" 才能访问，所以对于每个 Canvas 文件，你至少要保存三个信息：调用 Canvas 工具时提供的 **明确受众**: 你的读者是一个**即将接手你工作的同事**，他对项目一无所知。你的文档必须清晰到他不需要阅读任何原始对话就能继续工作。 你的工作记忆是有限的。为了有效管理，当上下文过长时，系统将启动一个记忆压缩周期。请严格遵循以下规则： 1. **触发**：你**必须**立即调用 `WorkingMemoryRewriteTool`，将总结后的工作记忆保存到 `ModelNote` 中。 2. **自动遗忘**：在你使用该工具更新 `ModelNote` 后，系统将自动丢弃工作记忆中最旧的部分以释放空间。此步骤是自动的，你无需执行任何操作。 3. **恢复计划**：将所有必要信息保存到 `ModelNote` 至关重要。 4. **关键规则**：**绝不伪造（杜撰）已遗忘的信息。**","moa_comms_createSubAgentGuidelines":"在 MoA 体系下创建一个 已经开始执行任务，完成后会进行通知，无需继续调用更多工具","proTask_firstMeet_intro_privacyNotice_web":"当你使用任务模式时，豆包可能会临时调用当前浏览器、代表你访问特定网站并进行相关操作（包括点击、输入等）；部分操作（如登录、账号安全验证）可能仍需你根据提示接管操作。AI 可能有误操作风险，请谨慎使用。豆包仅在执行任务所必须时才操作页面，任务执行中的具体动作将通过任务执行界面实时展示，你也可以随时终止任务。\n当你使用电脑端时，为提升任务执行效率与用户体验，针对需要登录后使用的网站，如你近期已在当前豆包浏览器登录，且登录 Cookie 未过期的，豆包将通过浏览器本地缓存的历史 Cookie 信息自动访问并执行操作。Cookie 信息仅在本地调用。若你不希望豆包依赖登录凭证直接访问的，可以终止本次任务或通过浏览器右上角菜单里的“删除浏览数据 > Cookie 及其他网站数据”来清除浏览 Cookie。清除后，当次任务如涉及需登录网站，无论本地是否登录均需你手动完成登录。\n当你使用网页端时，可以打开“设置 > 任务模式下，自动保存 Cookie”开关，让豆包自动调用缓存 Cookie 信息从而提升体验。在你打开此开关后，可以随时关闭开关和清除 Cookie。","proTask_firstMeet_intro_privacyNotice_web_global":"当你使用专家模型时，Dola 可能会临时调用当前浏览器、代表你访问特定网站并进行相关操作（包括点击、输入等）；部分操作（如登录、账号安全验证）可能仍需你根据提示接管操作。AI 可能有误操作风险，请谨慎使用。Dola 依赖登录凭证直接访问的，可以终止本次任务或通过浏览器右上角菜单里的“删除浏览数据 > Cookie 及其他网站数据”来清除浏览 Cookie。清除后，当次任务如涉及需登录网站，无论本地是否登录均需你手动完成登录。当你使用网页端时，可以打开“设置 > 隐私与权限 > 专家模型下，自动保存 Cookie”开关，让 Dola 自动调用缓存 Cookie 信息从而提升体验。在你打开此开关后，可以随时关闭开关和清除 Cookie。","proTask_mainAgent_preResponse_cn":"任务已经开始在做了，等完成后我再通知你","proTask_mainAgent_preResponse_init_reminder_cn":"任务已经开始，请回复用户任务开始的相关信息包括任务名称（其他字段如 organizer_id 等无需告诉用户 可能有误操作风险，请谨慎。\n为提升任务执行效率与用户体验，针对需要登录后使用的网站，如你近期已在当前豆包电脑版登录过该网站且登录 Cookies 未过期的，豆包将通过浏览器本地缓存的历史 Cookies 信息自动访问并执行操作。Cookies 信息仅在本地调用。若你不希望豆包依赖登录凭证直接访问的，可以终止本次任务或者通过【豆包右上角头像-清除浏览数据- Cookies 及其他网站数据】清除浏览 可能有误操作风险，请谨慎使用。豆包仅在执行任务所必须时才操作页面，任务执行中的具体动作将通过任务执行界面实时展示，你也可以随时终止任务。\n当你使用电脑端时，为提升任务执行效率与用户体验，针对需要登录后使用的网站，如你近期已在当前豆包浏览器登录，且登录 Cookie 未过期的，豆包将通过浏览器本地缓存的历史 Cookie 信息自动访问并执行操作。Cookie 信息仅在本地调用。若你不希望豆包依赖登录凭证直接访问的，可以终止本次任务或通过浏览器右上角菜单里的“删除浏览数据 > Cookie 及其他网站数据”来清除浏览 Cookie。清除后，当次任务如涉及需登录网站，无论本地是否登录均需你手动完成登录。\n当你使用网页端时，可以打开“设置 > 超能模式下，自动保存 Cookie”开关，让豆包自动调用缓存 Cookie a given bash command and returns its output.\n\nThe working directory persists between commands, but shell state does not. Environment variables, aliases, functions, and shell options set in one Bash call do NOT carry over to the next call. Use absolute paths instead of relying on variables / `cd` aliases set in a prior turn.\n\nIMPORTANT: Avoid using this tool to run `find`, `grep`, `cat`, `head`, `tail`, `sed`, `awk`, or `echo` commands, unless explicitly instructed. Instead, use the appropriate dedicated tool:\n - File search: Use Glob (NOT find or ls)\n - Content search: Use Grep (NOT grep or rg)\n - Read files: Use Read (NOT cat/head/tail)\n - Edit files: Use Edit (NOT sed/awk)\n - Write files: Use Write (NOT echo >/cat <<EOF)\n\n### Instructions\n - If your command will create new directories or files, first run `ls` to verify the parent directory exists.\n - Always quote file paths that contain spaces with double quotes.\n - You may specify an optional timeout in milliseconds (up to 600000ms / 10 minutes). Default timeout is 120000ms (2 minutes).\n - Use `run_in_background: true` for long-running commands. This is the only way to obtain a tracked `backgroundTaskId` you can pass to TaskOutput / TaskStop. Manually appending `&` runs the command in the V25 session (no SIGHUP), but you lose the task id and cannot reliably wait on it.\n - Foreground commands that exceed ~15 seconds are automatically moved to the background and you''ll receive a `backgroundTaskId`; use `TaskOutput(task_id, block=true, timeout=<ms>)` to wait for completion.\n - When issuing multiple commands: use ''&&'' to chain dependent commands, or make multiple Bash tool calls for independent commands.","tool_groups.edit":"Performs exact string replacements in files.\n\nUsage:\n- You must use your Read tool at least once before editing.\n- Preserve the exact indentation (tabs/spaces) from the file.\n- ALWAYS prefer editing existing files. NEVER write new files unless explicitly required.\n- The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Provide more surrounding context to make it unique, or use `replace_all`.\n- Use `replace_all` for renaming variables across the file.","tool_groups.glob":"Fast file pattern matching tool that works with any codebase size.\n- Supports glob patterns like ''**/*.js'' or ''src/**/*.ts''\n- Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time\n- Use this tool when you need to find files by name patterns\n- You can call multiple Glob tools in a single response for batch searching.","tool_groups.grep":"A powerful search tool built on ripgrep.\n\nUsage:\n- ALWAYS use Grep for search tasks. NEVER invoke `grep` or `rg` as a Bash command.\n- Supports full regex syntax (e.g., ''log.*Error'', ''function\\s+\\w+'')\n- Filter files with glob parameter (e.g., ''*.js'') or type parameter (e.g., ''py'')\n- Output modes: ''content'' shows matching lines, ''files_with_matches'' shows file paths (default), ''count'' shows match counts\n- Multiline matching: use `multiline: true` for cross-line patterns","tool_groups.item_13":"Keep a sandbox instance alive in the background after the task ends (instances are otherwise auto-reclaimed). Provide the instance id and a reason. Fails when the background sandbox limit (5) is already reached, returning the currently kept sandboxes (id, reason, how long they have existed) so you can drop one with SandboxDrop and retry.","tool_groups.item_14":"Retrieves output from a running or completed background task.\\n- Takes a task_id parameter identifying the task\\n- Use block=true (default) to wait until the task completes or timeout ms elapses (whichever comes first); default timeout is 30000 ms, max 600000 ms\\n- Use block=false for a non-blocking single-snapshot status check\\n- Works with both explicit Bash(run_in_background=true) task ids and the backgroundTaskId returned when a foreground command was auto-moved to the background (long-running commands beyond the assistant''s blocking budget)","tool_groups.item_15":"Stops a running background task by its ID.\n- Returns a success or failure status\n- Use when you need to terminate a long-running background task","tool_groups.item_17":"Writes a file to the local filesystem.\\n\\nUsage:\\n- This tool will overwrite the existing file if there is one at the provided path.\\n- If this is an existing file, you MUST use the Read tool first.\\n- Prefer the Edit tool for modifying existing files — it only sends the diff.\\n- Only use this tool to create new files or for complete rewrites.","tool_groups.item_18":"Finish the Local File task and provide the final answer.","tool_groups.item_19":"Ask the user a free-form question. The user reply (or skip) is surfaced via the QuickReplyBlock scene:2 UI.","tool_groups.item_2":"Batch-upload one or more local sandbox files to the public network and return file tokens. Input: path_list (array of absolute paths, max 50).","tool_groups.item_20":"Ask the user to authorize additional Local File folder access.","tool_groups.item_21":"Ask the user to perform a manual action (browser takeover, Windows PIP operation, file upload, or PIP environment initialization). Required params: \"type\" and \"display_message\". Use {computerEnvInitUserRequestActionTypePrompt} when computer_env_init reports the PIP system environment needs initialization; use {pipUserRequestActionTypePrompt} for Windows PIP login or desktop-operation help; use type=browserControl only for browser takeover; type=fileUpload for file upload. \"display_message\" is the user-facing guide text — it renders as the on-screen guidance and the desktop notification body. Surfaces a QuickReplyBlock scene:2 with action items.","tool_groups.item_22":"Ask the user to confirm a high-risk Local File operation.","tool_groups.item_6":"Replaces the contents of a specific cell in a Jupyter notebook (.ipynb file). The notebook_path must be an absolute path. Cells are identified by cell_id (not cell_number). Use edit_mode=insert to add a new cell (inserted after cell_id, or appended to the end of the notebook if cell_id is omitted). Use edit_mode=delete to remove a cell.","tool_groups.item_8":"Drop (destroy and reclaim) a background sandbox instance by its instance id. Use this to free a slot when the background sandbox limit is reached.","tool_groups.read":"Reads a file from the local filesystem.\\n\\nUsage:\\n- The file_path parameter must be an absolute path, not a relative path\\n- By default, it reads the whole file; for large files use offset/limit to paginate\\n- offset and limit are TOKEN-based (unit: estimated tokens via bbpe155k tokenizer; English 1 token ≈ 4 chars, Chinese 1 token ≈ 1.7 chars). Do NOT confuse with line count. Passing limit=20 returns ~20 tokens (a few words), NOT 20 lines. Default limit is 4000 tokens; omit limit entirely unless you need pagination.\\n- This tool can read Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb files) and images\\n- For PDF files, use the pages parameter to specify a page range (e.g. \"1-5\")\\n- This tool can only read files, not directories. To read a directory, use ls via Bash.\\n- You can call multiple Read tools in a single response for batch reading.","tool_groups.sandboxenvactivate":"Activate a prepared Local File sandbox
